title: An Indian Summer Day on the Prairie
content:
(In the Beginning)

         The sun is a huntress young,
         The sun is a red, red joy,
         The sun is an Indian girl,
         Of the tribe of the Illinois.


                 (Mid-morning)

         The sun is a smouldering fire,
         That creeps through the high gray plain,
         And leaves not a bush of cloud
         To blossom with flowers of rain.


                 (Noon)

         The sun is a wounded deer,
         That treads pale grass in the skies,
         Shaking his golden horns,
         Flashing his baleful eyes.


                 (Sunset)

         The sun is an eagle old,
         There in the windless west.
         Atop of the spirit-cliffs
         He builds him a crimson nest.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Vachel Lindsay delivers a powerful performance in "An Indian Summer Day on the Prairie"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
